title: Restore e-commerce features and fix Admin page loading

requirements.text: Restore the CartContext provider in frontend/src/context/CartContext.tsx with full cart state management (addToCart, removeFromCart, updateQuantity, clearCart, cartTotal). Ensure the CartContext provider wraps the entire app in frontend/src/App.tsx so all pages have access to cart state.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: CartContext.tsx exists at frontend/src/context/CartContext.tsx and exports CartContext and CartProvider
CartProvider wraps the app in App.tsx
addToCart, removeFromCart, updateQuantity, clearCart, and cartTotal are accessible via useCart hook
Cart state persists across page navigation within the session

requirements.text: Restore the Cart page at frontend/src/pages/Cart.tsx accessible at the route '/cart'. It must list all cart items with product name, variant name, unit price (in ₹), editable quantity with increment/decrement controls, line total, and a remove button per item. Display the cart subtotal. Include a 'Proceed to Checkout' button navigating to '/checkout' and a 'Continue Shopping' link to '/products'. Show an empty cart message with a link to Products when the cart is empty.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: Cart page renders at route '/cart'
Cart items show product name, variant name, unit price in ₹, quantity selector, line total, and remove button
Cart subtotal is displayed
'Proceed to Checkout' navigates to '/checkout'
'Continue Shopping' navigates to '/products'
Empty cart state shows a message and link to Products

requirements.text: Restore the Checkout page at frontend/src/pages/Checkout.tsx accessible at the route '/checkout'. It must display a form for buyer details (Name, Email, Phone Number) and a Shipping Address (Street, City, State, Pincode, Country), an order summary sidebar with cart items, quantities, and total amount in ₹. On form submission, call the usePlaceOrder mutation hook to submit the order to the backend. On success, clear the cart and navigate to '/order-confirmation/:orderId'.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: Checkout page renders at route '/checkout'
Buyer details form includes Name, Email, Phone Number fields
Shipping Address form includes Street, City, State, Pincode, Country fields
Order summary sidebar lists cart items with quantities and total in ₹
Form submission calls usePlaceOrder mutation
On success, cart is cleared and user is redirected to '/order-confirmation/:orderId'

requirements.text: Restore the Order Confirmation page at frontend/src/pages/OrderConfirmation.tsx accessible at the route '/order-confirmation/:orderId'. It must fetch and display order details (order ID, buyer info, status, shipping address, items) using useGetOrderById, and display a thank-you message.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: Order Confirmation page renders at route '/order-confirmation/:orderId'
Page fetches order by ID from the backend via useGetOrderById
Displays order ID, buyer name, status, shipping address, and order items
Thank-you message is shown prominently

requirements.text: Restore the 'Add to Cart' functionality on the ProductDetail page (frontend/src/pages/ProductDetail.tsx). Ensure a variant selector pre-populated with the product's variants, a quantity selector defaulting to 1 with increment/decrement controls, and an 'Add to Cart' button that calls addToCart from CartContext using the selected variant's name, unitPrice from the product's price field, and the current quantity. Display a success toast notification when an item is added. Do not alter existing product image, specifications, nutritional information, or product variants display sections.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: Variant selector is populated from backend product data
Quantity selector defaults to 1 and allows increment/decrement
'Add to Cart' button calls addToCart with correct productId, productName, variantName, unitPrice, and quantity
Success toast notification is displayed after adding to cart
Existing product image, specifications, nutritional information, and variant display sections are unchanged

requirements.text: Restore the cart icon button in the Navigation component (frontend/src/components/Navigation.tsx) that displays the current number of items in the cart as a badge. Clicking it must navigate to '/cart'. Do not alter the existing logo size (h-24), navigation links, or mobile menu structure.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: Cart icon is visible in the navigation header on both desktop and mobile layouts
Cart icon badge shows the current number of items in the cart
Clicking the cart icon navigates to '/cart'
Logo size (h-24), navigation links, and mobile menu structure are unchanged

requirements.text: Ensure the React Query hooks for e-commerce are present and correct in frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: usePlaceOrder (mutation calling placeOrder), useGetAllOrders (query calling getAllOrders), useGetOrderById (query calling getOrderById), and useUpdateOrderStatus (mutation calling updateOrderStatus). Verify each hook correctly interfaces with the backend actor methods.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: usePlaceOrder mutation hook exists and calls backend placeOrder
useGetAllOrders query hook exists and calls backend getAllOrders
useGetOrderById query hook exists and calls backend getOrderById with an orderId parameter
useUpdateOrderStatus mutation hook exists and calls backend updateOrderStatus
All hooks handle loading and error states

requirements.text: Register the '/cart', '/checkout', and '/order-confirmation/$orderId' routes in frontend/src/App.tsx by adding the corresponding route definitions and lazy-loading the Cart, Checkout, and OrderConfirmation page components. Do not alter any existing routes or layout structure.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: Route '/cart' renders the Cart page component
Route '/checkout' renders the Checkout page component
Route '/order-confirmation/$orderId' renders the OrderConfirmation page component
All existing routes remain unchanged
New page components are lazy-loaded

requirements.text: Ensure the backend Motoko actor (backend/main.mo) contains the complete e-commerce data model and all required functions: Order type with orderId, buyerName, buyerEmail, buyerPhone, shippingAddress, items (array of OrderItem with productId, productName, variantName, quantity, unitPrice), totalAmount, status (pending/confirmed/processing/shipped/delivered/cancelled), and createdAt. Stable storage for orders. Public functions: placeOrder, getOrderById, getAllOrders, and updateOrderStatus. If any of these are missing or broken, restore them fully.

quotes: E-commerce feature is removed, check it and add it

acceptanceCriteria: Order and OrderItem types are defined in main.mo
Stable storage variable for orders exists
placeOrder function accepts buyer info, shipping address, and cart items and returns the created order
getOrderById returns a specific order by orderId
getAllOrders returns all orders for admin use
updateOrderStatus updates an order's status by orderId
All functions compile without errors

requirements.text: Diagnose and fix the Admin page (frontend/src/pages/Admin.tsx) not loading. Investigate likely causes including missing or broken imports (CartContext, useGetAllOrders, useUpdateOrderStatus), TypeScript type errors, or missing route registration. Ensure the Admin page renders both the 'Contact Submissions' tab and the 'Orders' tab correctly, with the Orders tab fetching data via useGetAllOrders and status updates via useUpdateOrderStatus.

quotes: also the admin page not loading check that as well

acceptanceCriteria: Admin page renders without errors at its route
'Contact Submissions' tab displays all contact form submissions with Name, Email, Phone, Message, and User Type
'Orders' tab displays all orders with Order ID, Buyer Name, Email, Phone, Total Amount (₹), Status, and Date
Status update dropdown per order row calls useUpdateOrderStatus when changed
No TypeScript or runtime errors on page load

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
Do not alter the existing logo size (h-24 in Navigation, h-20 in Footer)
Do not alter existing navigation links or mobile menu structure
Do not modify the existing Contact Submissions display in the Admin page
Do not alter existing product image, specifications, nutritional information, or product variants display sections on ProductDetail page
All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
nonGoals: Adding new product categories beyond the existing six
Changing the visual design or color palette
Modifying the watermark or logo assets
Real-time order tracking or WebSocket features
Changing existing page layouts other than restoring removed e-commerce components
